design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate horizontally scrolling stages, manage four switchable weapons with upgrade pickups, survive enemy patterns and bosses across six levels with increasing difficulty
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (weapon selection and upgrades) combined with pattern avoidance and score-chasing for extra lives
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot advanced spacecraft (CNCS1) as Captain Lancer on a surprise attack against robot dictator Super Mech and the Black Nebula threat in 2998
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Fixed weapon direction regardless of player movement creates positioning puzzle distinct from typical horizontal shooters; Genesis version adds bomb mechanic and satellite option, expanding tactical depth beyond arcade version

difficulty.lives_system: Lives lost on enemy fire hit or collision with stage obstacles; extra lives obtained by collecting 'B' icons for points; firepower and speed reset to default on death penalty
difficulty.continues_system: Credit-based continue system; player can insert additional credits to continue after game over
difficulty.checkpointing: Arcade version supports configurable checkpoint or respawn system—downed player either restarts at last checkpoint reached or immediately respawns at death location
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Score milestones gate extra lives; checkpoint/respawn mode configurable in arcade version; game loops after completion with increased enemy bullet density

progression.upgrade_system: Four main weapons available from start; upgraded via 'P' icon pickups scattered through levels; player switches between weapons using change button; each weapon fires in fixed direction independent of player movement
progression.unlock_mechanism: Six sequential stages unlocked by defeating stage bosses; subsequent loops increase difficulty and enemy fire patterns
replayability_hooks: Score chasing through 'B' icon collection for extra lives
Multiple weapon strategy experimentation across six levels
Looping difficulty escalation with denser bullet patterns
Genesis version exclusive: bomb, shield, and satellite option mechanics add replay variation

world_building_techniques: Year 2998 setting with intergalactic travel and space colonization backdrop
Antagonist framing: Super Mech as robot dictator threatening human-controlled galaxies via Black Nebula
Named protagonist (Captain Lancer) and spacecraft (CNCS1) with titular weapon (Hellfire)
Version-specific narrative variance: arcade/Genesis ending shows safe return to Earth; PC Engine CD-ROM² features self-sacrifice ending for character Kaoru

boss_design.philosophy: One boss per stage (six total); bosses serve as mandatory progression gates requiring pattern learning and adaptive weapon selection
notable_bosses: Super Mech (final antagonist and presumed final stage boss)
Black Nebula (space matter threat, likely tied to stage encounters)

faction_indicators: Space Federation (player faction, represented by Captain Lancer)
danger_signals: Black Nebula spreading and engulfing stars
Robot dictator Super Mech commanding armada across increasingly difficult levels
Escalating enemy fire density in looping difficulty
environmental_storytelling: Scrolling backgrounds depicting stages under Black Nebula threat
Enemy and boss hand-drawn pixel art designs establish visual threat hierarchy
Stage progression from outer space territories toward Super Mech stronghold
